structure_label: Pronoun order: me, te, se, nous, vous before other pronouns
core_meaning: Reflexive and personal pronouns precede other pronoun objects in the verbal chain
primary_communicative_fn: Establishing correct pronoun sequencing with personal pronouns first
when_to_use: When using multiple pronouns with a verb, place me/te/se/nous/vous before other objects
key_variations: Il me le donne (He gives it to me).
Elle te la montre (She shows it to you).
Nous vous l'avons dit (We told it to you).
Ils se les sont appropriés (They took them for themselves).
essential_vocabulary_slots: donner, montrer, dire, approprier, envoyer, prêter, offrir
common_learner_mistakes: Placing direct object pronoun before reflexive: *« le me donne » instead of « il me le donne »
Reversing the order of personal and direct object pronouns
Treating all pronouns as equivalent in ordering
chunk_compatibility: Complex pronoun sequences in routine interactions
primary_function: Establishing pronoun hierarchy and correct ordering rules
essential_vocabulary: donner, montrer, dire, envoyer, prêter, offrir, vendre, apporter
why_it_matters: Personal pronouns take precedence in pronoun chains—foundational ordering rule

structure_label: Pronoun order: double object pronouns (le/la/les before lui/leur)
core_meaning: When both a direct (le/la/les) and indirect (lui/leur) object pronoun are used, direct object precedes indirect object
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering direct and indirect object pronouns correctly
when_to_use: When two pronouns represent object and recipient, place direct object first
key_variations: Je le lui donne (I give it to him).
Elle la leur a prêtée (She lent it to them).
Nous le leur avons expliqué (We explained it to them).
Ils la lui ont présentée (They introduced her to him).
essential_vocabulary_slots: donner, prêter, expliquer, présenter, montrer, envoyer, vendre
common_learner_mistakes: Reversing the order: *« je lui le donne » instead of « je le lui donne »
Placing indirect object before direct object
Confusing COD/COI pronouns in complex structures
chunk_compatibility: Indirect and direct object pronoun chains in exchanges
essential_vocabulary: donner, prêter, expliquer, présenter, montrer, envoyer, vendre, apporter
why_it_matters: Direct object pronouns precede indirect in standard French ordering

structure_label: Pronoun order with « en » and « y »
core_meaning: « En » and « y » occupy the final position in the pronoun chain
primary_communicative_fn: Placing partitive and locative pronouns at chain's end
when_to_use: When using en/y with other pronouns, always place them last
key_variations: Je m'en souviens (I remember it/that).
Elle s'y intéresse (She's interested in it).
Nous nous en doutions (We suspected it).
Ils s'y attendent (They expect it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: souvenir, intéresser, douter, attendre, parler, besoin, avoir
common_learner_mistakes: Placing « en » before other pronouns: *« en me souviens » instead of « je m'en souviens »
Putting y or en before reflexive pronouns
Treating en/y as equivalent to other pronouns in ordering
chunk_compatibility: Partitive and locative references with pronoun chains
primary_function: Establishing en/y as final pronouns in pronoun sequences
essential_vocabulary: souvenir, intéresser, douter, attendre, parler, avoir, besoin, penser
why_it_matters: En and y always conclude pronoun chains—their position never varies

core_meaning: In affirmative imperative, pronouns follow the verb in the order: le/la/les → lui/leur → y → en
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering pronouns after imperative verbs with postposition
when_to_use: In affirmative commands, pronouns follow verb and are hyphenated
pattern: Verbe + le/la/les + lui/leur + y + en (with hyphens)
key_variations: Donne-le-moi (Give it to me).
Dis-leur-en (Tell them about it).
Prête-la-lui (Lend it to him).
Parle-lui-en (Talk to him about it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: donner, dire, prêter, parler, envoyer, montrer, apporter
common_learner_mistakes: Using negative imperative order: *« donne-moi-le » instead of « donne-le-moi »
Forgetting hyphens between verb and pronouns
Placing pronouns before verb in affirmative commands
chunk_compatibility: Direct commands with multiple pronoun recipients
primary_function: Establishing postposition pronoun ordering in imperatives
key_forms: Verbe + direct object + indirect object + y + en (all hyphenated)
essential_vocabulary: donner, dire, prêter, parler, envoyer, montrer, apporter, porter
why_it_matters: Affirmative imperatives reverse normal pronoun order—follows verb

core_meaning: In negative imperative, pronouns precede the verb in the same order as with conjugated verbs
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering pronouns before verbs in negative commands
when_to_use: In negative commands, pronouns stay before verb, not after
key_variations: Ne le lui donne pas (Don't give it to him).
Ne leur en parle pas (Don't talk to them about it).
Ne la lui prête pas (Don't lend it to him).
Ne m'en parle pas (Don't talk to me about it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: donner, parler, prêter, dire, montrer, envoyer, faire
common_learner_mistakes: Using postposition order from affirmative imperatives: *« ne donne-le-pas »
Placing pronouns after verb in negative commands
Reversing pronoun order between positive and negative
chunk_compatibility: Negative instructions with multiple pronoun referents
primary_function: Maintaining pronoun precedence in negative imperatives
essential_vocabulary: donner, parler, prêter, dire, montrer, envoyer, faire, apporter
why_it_matters: Negative imperatives keep standard pronoun positioning before verbs

core_meaning: Full ordering: me/te/se/nous/vous (1) → le/la/les (2) → lui/leur (3) → y (4) → en (5)
primary_communicative_fn: Establishing complete pronoun hierarchy for all chain positions
when_to_use: When using multiple pronouns, follow strict ordering based on pronoun type
key_variations: Il me le lui donne (He gives it to him for me).
Elle nous la leur a envoyée (She sent it to them for us).
Je m'y intéresse depuis longtemps (I've been interested in it for a long time).
Ils s'en sont aperçus (They became aware of it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: donner, envoyer, intéresser, apercevoir, dire, montrer, offrir
common_learner_mistakes: Incorrect ordering of pronoun types
Forgetting « s' » before « en »: *« il se en est aperçu » instead of « il s'en est aperçu »
Treating pronoun positions as flexible or interchangeable
chunk_compatibility: Complex pronoun stacking in multi-object interactions
primary_function: Establishing complete pronoun ordering hierarchy (five-position rule)
essential_vocabulary: donner, envoyer, intéresser, apercevoir, dire, montrer, offrir, faire
why_it_matters: Pronoun hierarchy is fixed—understanding all five positions prevents errors

structure_label: Pronoun order in passé composé with reflexive verb
core_meaning: With reflexive verbs in passé composé, the past participle agrees with the direct object pronoun
primary_communicative_fn: Managing past participle agreement in reflexive constructions
when_to_use: With reflexive passé composé, agree past participle with the COD (often the reflexive pronoun)
pattern: auxiliaire (être) + pronom réfléchi + autre pronom (si applicable) | participe passé accordé
key_variations: Elle s'est levée (She got up - agrees with feminine 'elle').
Ils se sont regardés (They looked at each other - plural agreement).
Nous nous sommes habillés (We got dressed - plural agreement).
Je me suis habillée (I got dressed - feminine agreement).
essential_vocabulary_slots: lever, regarder, habiller, laver, réveiller, coucher, approcher
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting agreement: *« elle s'est levé » instead of « elle s'est levée »
Treating reflexive as passive instead of true reflexive
Not agreeing when reflexive pronoun is the direct object
chunk_compatibility: Reflexive routine actions in past tense with agreement requirements
primary_function: Managing past participle agreement in reflexive passé composé
essential_vocabulary: lever, regarder, habiller, laver, réveiller, coucher, approcher, souvenir
why_it_matters: Reflexive passé composé always uses être and requires agreement

structure_label: Pronoun order with verbs followed by « à » (lui/leur)
core_meaning: Verbs of giving, saying, and showing use lui/leur as indirect objects
primary_communicative_fn: Using indirect object pronouns for dative verbs
when_to_use: With verbs taking 'à', use lui/leur not stressed pronouns after preposition
key_variations: Je lui ai offert un cadeau (I offered him a gift).
Elle leur a écrit une lettre (She wrote them a letter).
Nous le leur avons démontré (We demonstrated it to them).
Ils la lui ont présentée (They introduced her to him).
essential_vocabulary_slots: offrir, écrire, démontrer, présenter, dire, donner, montrer
common_learner_mistakes: Using stressed pronouns after preposition: *« j'ai donné à lui » instead of « je lui ai donné »
Confusing dative 'à' verbs with prepositions requiring stressed pronouns
Placing à + pronoun after the infinitif instead of using lui/leur
chunk_compatibility: Giving, showing, and telling actions with indirect recipients
primary_function: Managing indirect object pronouns with dative verbs
key_forms: lui/leur (not à lui/à elle/à eux/à elles)
essential_vocabulary: offrir, écrire, dire, donner, montrer, envoyer, démontrer, présenter
why_it_matters: Lui/leur replace à + pronoun in pronoun chains—critical distinction

structure_label: Pronoun order with verbs followed by « de » (en)
core_meaning: Verbs and expressions requiring « de » use en for quantities or topics
primary_communicative_fn: Using partitive pronouns with verbs requiring de
when_to_use: With verbs taking 'de', use en to replace quantities or partitive meanings
key_variations: J'en ai besoin (I need some of it).
Elle s'en souvient (She remembers it).
Nous lui en avons parlé (We talked to him about it).
Nous en avons discuté (We discussed it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: besoin, souvenir, parler, discuter, avoir, penser, avoir peur
common_learner_mistakes: Omitting « en » with partitive verbs: *« j'ai besoin » without « en »
Confusing verb requirements for « de » vs « à »
Placing en before other pronouns in the chain
chunk_compatibility: Topic and quantity references with verbs requiring de
primary_function: Using en for quantities and topics with de-verbs
essential_vocabulary: besoin, souvenir, parler, discuter, avoir, penser, avoir peur, être sûr
why_it_matters: En replaces de + pronoun and takes final position in chains

structure_label: Stressed pronouns after prepositions (moi, toi, lui, elle, etc.)
core_meaning: Prepositions require stressed pronoun forms when referring to people
when_to_use: After all prepositions (pour, sans, chez, avec, etc.), use stressed pronouns
key_variations: C'est pour moi (It's for me).
Il est parti sans elle (He left without her).
Nous comptons sur eux (We count on them).
Elle discute avec vous (She discusses with you).
essential_vocabulary_slots: pour, sans, chez, avec, sur, entre, vers, derrière
common_learner_mistakes: Using subject pronouns after prepositions: *« pour je » instead of « pour moi »
Using tonic pronouns as subjects: *« moi suis » instead of « je suis »
Confusing subject and stressed pronoun forms
chunk_compatibility: Location and companionship expressions with stressed pronouns
primary_function: Using stressed pronouns after all prepositions
key_forms: moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, eux, elles
essential_vocabulary: pour, sans, chez, avec, sur, entre, vers, derrière, avant, après
why_it_matters: Stressed pronouns are obligatory after all prepositions—cannot use subject forms

structure_label: Pronoun order with compound tenses and double pronouns
core_meaning: In compound tenses, past participle agreements follow specific rules based on pronouns
primary_communicative_fn: Managing past participle agreement with multiple pronouns
when_to_use: Agree past participle when direct object pronoun precedes the verb
pattern: avoir/être + pronom(s) + participe passé accordé selon le COD qui précède
key_variations: Je l'ai vue (agrees with 'la' = vue).
Je lui ai parlé (no agreement, 'lui' is indirect object).
Nous les avons pris (agrees with 'les' = pris).
Elle s'est couchée (agrees with 'se' = elle).
essential_vocabulary_slots: voir, parler, prendre, coucher, lever, habiller, donner
common_learner_mistakes: Over-agreeing with indirect objects: *« je lui ai parlée » instead of « je lui ai parlé »
Confusing COD (direct) and COI (indirect) for agreement purposes
Not agreeing when direct object precedes
chunk_compatibility: Past tense expressions with multiple pronoun dependencies
primary_function: Managing agreement rules with direct and indirect object pronouns
key_forms: Agreement only with direct objects that precede the verb
essential_vocabulary: voir, parler, prendre, coucher, lever, habiller, donner, envoyer
why_it_matters: Agreement rules differ for direct vs indirect objects—critical distinction

structure_label: Pronoun order: « ce » as object pronoun
core_meaning: « Ce » (that/it in impersonal contexts) is invariable, never agrees
primary_communicative_fn: Using invariable ce pronoun for abstract references
when_to_use: Use ce to refer to ideas, statements, or abstract concepts
pattern: verbe + ce (+ autres pronoms si applicable)
key_variations: C'est intéressant (That's interesting).
Je le crois (I believe it).
Elle l'a dit (She said it).
Nous le pensons (We think it).
essential_vocabulary_slots: être, croire, dire, penser, vouloir, savoir, comprendre
common_learner_mistakes: Treating « ce » as variable: *« ces », « cet », etc. in pronoun position
Confusing « ce » with « se » in reflexive contexts
Attempting to agree ce with antecedent
chunk_compatibility: Abstract references and opinions with invariable pronouns
primary_function: Using invariable ce for abstract and impersonal references
essential_vocabulary: être, croire, dire, penser, vouloir, savoir, comprendre, trouver
why_it_matters: Ce is always invariable—never changes for gender or number

core_meaning: In causative construction, pronouns replace the subject of infinitive (not the object)
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering pronouns in causative faire structures
when_to_use: When faire + infinitif, pronouns replace the subject being made to do something
pattern: faire + [pronom objet du causatif] + infinitif (+ complément si applicable)
key_variations: Je lui ai fait ranger sa chambre (I made him tidy his room).
Elle les a fait venir (She had them come).
Nous l'avons fait travailler dur (We made him work hard).
Ils l'ont fait construire (They had it built).
essential_vocabulary_slots: faire, ranger, venir, travailler, construire, partir, manger
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing the causative subject with the object
Not following pronoun ordering rules in causative chains
Treating faire + infinitif as simple faire conjugation
primary_function: Managing pronoun positioning in causative faire + infinitif
essential_vocabulary: faire, ranger, venir, travailler, construire, partir, manger, apprendre
why_it_matters: Causative faire requires special pronoun handling—subject becomes object

structure_label: Pronoun order: « voir », « entendre », « sentir » + infinitive
core_meaning: Verbs of perception with infinitive keep the same pronoun rules
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering pronouns with perception verbs and infinitives
when_to_use: With voir/entendre/sentir + infinitif, place pronouns before the perception verb
pattern: verbe de perception + pronom objet + infinitif
key_variations: Je la vois travailler (I see her working).
Nous les entendons chanter (We hear them singing).
Elle l'a sentie passer (She felt her pass by).
Il les a vus arriver (He saw them arrive).
essential_vocabulary_slots: voir, entendre, sentir, travailler, chanter, passer, arriver
common_learner_mistakes: Not moving the noun object to pronoun position before infinitive
Confusing with causative « faire » construction
Placing pronouns after the infinitif instead of before verb
primary_function: Managing pronouns with perception verbs and infinitives
essential_vocabulary: voir, entendre, sentir, travailler, chanter, passer, arriver, tomber
why_it_matters: Perception verbs follow standard pronoun ordering before the main verb

structure_label: Pronoun order with multiple negation particles
core_meaning: Negative adverbs (plus, jamais, rien) require special placement with pronouns
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering pronouns in negative statements with adverbial negation
when_to_use: In negations with plus/jamais/rien, pronouns precede the negative adverb
key_variations: Je ne m'y intéresse plus (I'm no longer interested in it).
Elle n'en a jamais parlé (She never talked about it).
Nous ne lui avons rien dit (We told him nothing).
Ils ne la voient plus (They no longer see her).
essential_vocabulary_slots: intéresser, parler, dire, voir, faire, entendre, croire
common_learner_mistakes: Placing negative adverbs before pronouns: *« ne plus m'intéresse »
Forgetting first « ne » in standard negation
Confusing order of pronouns and negative particles
primary_function: Positioning pronouns correctly in adverbial negations
essential_vocabulary: intéresser, parler, dire, voir, faire, entendre, croire, penser
why_it_matters: Negative adverbs come after pronouns but before verbs—specific ordering
